title: Airframe Materials Review Meeting — 2023-08-22
text:
# Airframe Materials Review Meeting — 2023-08-22

**Space:** K200  
**Meeting type:** Technical Design Review  
**Attendees:** Tobias Wren (Airframe), Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE), Felix Herrera (Integration), Aisha Mbeki (Test), Nina Schulz (Quality)  
**Chair:** Tobias Wren  
**Minutes recorded by:** Felix Herrera

---

## Background

KES-042 (wing skin material trade study) was initiated following KES-045 (wing skin delamination under vibration loading identified during ground vibration test, June 2023). The original wing skin design (P-1008 / P-1009) used straight carbon fibre reinforced polymer (CFRP). The delamination occurred at the wing root bond line after 12 hours of simulated vibration at the motor frequency spectrum.

## Material Options Evaluated

Tobias Wren presented three options:

1. **CFRP (original, 0°/90° layup):** Failed — delamination at wing root under motor vibration after 12h
2. **CFRP (quasi-isotropic ±45° dominant layup):** Modelled to increase delamination resistance by 40% but adds ~15g per skin. Estimated to pass the 50h fatigue requirement.
3. **Hybrid carbon-aramid composite (CFRP weave + Kevlar® ±45° interlayer):** Adds ~25g per skin but increases impact resistance dramatically and the aramid interlayer provides crack arrest. Test data from KES-042 material coupon tests shows no delamination at 100h simulated vibration.

Dr. Voss noted that the mass penalty of Option 3 (+50g total for both skins) is within the system mass margin (340g margin per REV-631 review of DOC-401).

## Decision

**AGREED:** Change wing skin material from CFRP to hybrid carbon-aramid composite.

- Decision maker: Tobias Wren (Airframe lead), approved by Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE)
- Rationale: CFRP failed the vibration test (KES-045); aramid interlayer provides crack arrest and delamination resistance; mass penalty acceptable within system margin
- ECR to be raised: ECR-207 (Tobias Wren, covering P-1008 and P-1009)
- Structural spec to update: DOC-409 (Structural Specification)
- Drawing to update: DRW-502 (Wing Skin & Spar Assembly)
- Loads analysis to re-run: DOC-407 (Airframe Loads Analysis) to verify stiffness change impact

## Actions

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Raise ECR-207 for P-1008 and P-1009 material change | Tobias Wren | 2023-08-29 |
| A2 | Update DOC-409 Structural Spec for hybrid composite | Tobias Wren | 2023-09-30 |
| A3 | Re-run loads analysis for stiffness change, update DOC-407 | Tobias Wren | 2023-09-30 |
| A4 | Update DRW-502 with revised layup schedule | Tobias Wren | 2023-10-15 |
| A5 | Fabricate and test 3 wing skin samples to hybrid spec | Aisha Mbeki | 2023-11-01 |
